hops.context: Our design allows to modify fCNN or replace it by other end-to-end deep learning models
The input of deep learning module includes the patient’s age
the patient’s age (encoded as a one-hot vector of size 10)

question: In the context of the described deep learning module, how is the feature related to patient age utilized?

options.A: Patient age is stored as categorical data in the database before being used in the deep learning model.
options.B: Patient age is used as a feature encoded as a one-hot vector of size 10 within the fCNN.
options.C: Patient age is converted into a continuous variable before being processed by the deep learning module.
options.D: Patient age is directly inputted as a numerical value without any encoding in the deep learning module.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the context indicates that the patient's age is encoded as a one-hot vector of size 10. This encoding method transforms the age into a binary vector format which is then used as a feature within the fCNN, part of the deep learning module.
context: Our design allows to modify fCNN or replace it by other end-to-end deep learning models | The input of deep learning module includes the patient’s age | the patient’s age (encoded as a one-hot vector of size 10)
